fusion_raw.judge_raw:
```json
{
  "score": 7,
  "consensus": [
    "ISR-safe synchronization is required for shared state (e.g., SystemState) to prevent kernel panics on ESP32 dual-core architecture",
    "Safety manager must actively override motion outputs during fault states, not just evaluate state",
    "Lock contention from cross-core synchronization can cause Task Watchdog Timer (TWDT) resets",
    "Analog BLDC drive signals need hardware pull-down resistors and ESC fail-safe configuration to handle ESP32 crashes or resets",
    "State machine recovery from FAULT to NORMAL/ARMED must require explicit user acknowledgment to prevent automatic dangerous transitions"
  ],
  "unique": [
    "Round 1 specifically identified the exact methods (ingestion vs. evaluation) where the data race on SystemState occurs",
    "Round 2 highlighted that the linting configuration's 80-character line limits and namespace indentation rules are overly pedantic for embedded C++ safety-critical code",
    "Round 3 noted that the motion_mixer component must strictly gate its output based on safety state, not just pass through requests"
  ],
  "blind_spots": [
    "No review addressed the initialization order of safety-critical components during boot (e.g., ensuring safety manager initializes before motion mixer)",
    "No review considered the impact of FreeRTOS task priorities on the control loop's ability to meet real-time deadlines under fault conditions",
    "No review evaluated the watchdog configuration (timeout duration, task assignment) relative to the worst-case execution time of the control loop",
    "No review examined the ESC's specific behavior on signal loss (e.g., does it brake, coast, or hold last command?) beyond generic pull-down recommendations"
  ]
}
```
